title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
code: # Install eCOMET (uncomment if not installed) # install.packages("pak") # pak::pkg_install("Phytoecia/eCOMET", dependencies = TRUE)  library(ecomet) library(dplyr) library(stringr)

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: tutorial, demonstrate use eCOMET package analyzing metabolomics data treatment-based study treatment-based study? treatment-based study, compare metabolite profiles different treatment groups (e.g., control vs. treated). main goal identify differentially accumulated metabolites (DAMs) — compounds whose abundance changes significantly response treatment. Common downstream analyses include volcano plots, heatmaps, chemical class enrichment understand types metabolites respond treatment. Tutorial dataset example data represent metabolomics analysis Arabidopsis thaliana (Col-0) leaves attacked two different herbivores: files shipped eCOMET package can accessed via system.file().

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: eCOMET distributed small set example files tutorials can run immediately without downloading additional data.
code: # Locate tutorial data shipped with the eCOMET package data_dir <- system.file(   "extdata/tutorials/treatment_based",   package = "ecomet" ) stopifnot(nzchar(data_dir))  # fail loudly if package/data not installed  # Define file paths demo_feature          <- file.path(data_dir, "feature_table_demo.csv") demo_metadata         <- file.path(data_dir, "metadata_demo.csv") demo_sirius_formula   <- file.path(data_dir, "canopus_formula_summary.tsv") demo_sirius_structure <- file.path(data_dir, "structure_identifications.tsv") demo_dreams           <- file.path(data_dir, "dreams_sim_demo.csv") gls_db                <- file.path(data_dir, "custom_DB_glucosinolates.csv")

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: mmo (mass-spectrometry metabolomics object) central data structure eCOMET. stores feature abundances, sample metadata, annotations, analysis results named list. create mmo object, need minimum: Feature abundance table — exported MZmine (preferably “full feature table” format) Sample metadata — CSV identifying samples treatment groups Optionally, can later add: SIRIUS/CANOPUS annotations chemical classification Chemical similarity scores (DreaMS, cosine, MS2DeepScore) Custom compound database annotations

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: GetMZmineFeature() reads MZmine feature table metadata, links together. newly created mmo object contains three main components: 1) Feature abundance matrix — rows features (m/z + retention time), columns samples: 2) Feature information — m/z, retention time, feature-level metadata: 3) Sample metadata — treatment groups, sample masses, phenotype data:
code: mmo <- GetMZmineFeature(   mzmine_dir   = demo_feature,   metadata_dir = demo_metadata,   group_col    = 'group',     # column in metadata defining treatment groups   sample_col   = 'sample'     # column in metadata with sample file names ) mmo$feature_data mmo$feature_info mmo$metadata

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: statistical analysis, apply several preprocessing steps. step adds new element mmo object — original data never overwritten, can always go back.

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: Mass spectrometry data often contains zeros (undetected features). need handled log transformation fold-change calculations. ReplaceZero() adds imputed version feature table (mmo$imputed_feature_data). method = 'one': Replace 0 NA values 1 (simple, conservative) method = 'half_min': Replace half minimum non-zero value per feature
code: mmo <- ReplaceZero(mmo, method = 'one') head(mmo$imputed_feature_data)

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: metadata contains mass column (tissue mass mg), MassNormalization() adjusts peak areas differences sample mass: normalized = original * mean(mass) / sample_mass.
code: mmo <- MassNormalization(mmo) mmo$feature_data

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: eCOMET offers several normalization methods. adds new element mmo object:
code: mmo <- MeancenterNormalization(mmo) mmo <- LogNormalization(mmo) mmo <- ZNormalization(mmo)

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: can also convert feature abundance matrix binary presence/absence matrix. Features abundance threshold marked present (1), otherwise absent (0).
code: mmo <- FeaturePresence(mmo, threshold = 1)

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: SIRIUS provides computational annotations including molecular formula predictions compound class assignments via CANOPUS (NPC ClassyFire classifications). Note duplicates: SIRIUS may assign multiple candidate annotations feature. see duplicate feature entries mmo$sirius_annot, top-ranked annotation per feature retained. Check summary(mmo) confirm annotation count matches.
code: mmo <- AddSiriusAnnot(   mmo,   canopus_structuredir = demo_sirius_structure,   canopus_formuladir   = demo_sirius_formula ) mmo$sirius_annot

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: CANOPUS assigns posterior probabilities compound class prediction. can filter low-confidence predictions improve quality downstream enrichment analyses. First, let’s visualize distribution prediction confidence: typically long tail low-confidence predictions. can filter using filter_canopus_annotations(): pathway_level: classification levels filter (e.g., "NPC#pathway", "", "All_NPC") threshold: minimum probability retain (values set NA) suffix: label filtered result choose threshold? universal threshold. SIRIUS documentation recommends using context-dependent thresholds. statistical analyses, can sum probabilities get expected counts rather applying hard cutoff.
code: hist(mmo$sirius_annot$`NPC#pathway Probability`,      main = "Distribution of NPC Pathway Probabilities",      xlab = "Probability", col = "lightblue") mmo <- filter_canopus_annotations(   mmo,   pathway_level = "NPC#pathway",   threshold     = 0.8,   suffix        = "NPC_pathway_0.8",   overwrite     = TRUE ) mmo$sirius_annot_filtered_NPC_pathway_0.8

what: 4.3 Filter structure predictions by COSMIC confidence
title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: SIRIUS structure predictions scored COSMIC confidence score. scores interpreted caution — probabilities. SIRIUS team recommends focusing highest-confidence hits (e.g., top 5–10%).
code: # Replace -Infinity values with 0 mmo$sirius_annot_filtered_NPC_pathway_0.8$ConfidenceScoreApproximate[   which(mmo$sirius_annot_filtered_NPC_pathway_0.8$ConfidenceScoreApproximate == "-Infinity") ] <- 0  Cosmic_Scores <- as.numeric(   mmo$sirius_annot_filtered_NPC_pathway_0.8$ConfidenceScoreApproximate )  hist(Cosmic_Scores, main = "COSMIC Confidence Scores for Structures",      xlab = "Confidence Score", col = "lightblue")  # Find the threshold for the top 10% of scores quantile(x = Cosmic_Scores, probs = 0.9, na.rm = TRUE) mmo <- filter_cosmic_structure(   mmo,   input       = "sirius_annot_filtered_NPC_pathway_0.8",   cosmic_mode = "approx",   threshold   = 0.3892,   suffix      = "CANOPUS_0.8_COSMIC_Top_10" )

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: Using SIRIUS annotations, can create vectors features belonging specific compound classes. useful downstream analyses like targeted heatmaps PCA.
code: # Extract flavonoid features using ClassyFire annotation FLVs <- mmo$sirius_annot %>%   filter(str_detect(`ClassyFire#most specific class`, "Flavonoid")) %>%   pull(id) FLVs

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: can match features -house compound library m/z (ppm tolerance) retention time (minute tolerance).
code: mmo <- AddCustomAnnot(mmo, DB = gls_db, mztol = 5, rttol = 0.2)  # Extract glucosinolate features GLSs <- mmo$custom_annot %>%   filter(lengths(custom_annot) > 0) %>%   pull(id) GLSs

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: Chemical similarity scores (e.g., DreaMS) can used chemically-informed heatmap clustering diversity analyses.
code: mmo <- AddChemDist(mmo, dreams_dir = demo_dreams) mmo$dreams.dissim[1:10, 1:10]

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: PCA (Principal Component Analysis) plot visualizes overall distribution samples across groups. PCAplot() performs PCA, generates scatter plot confidence ellipses, runs PERMANOVA test check groups significantly different. Key options: normalization: normalization use ("None", "Log", "Meancentered", "Z") label: whether label individual sample points filter_id / id_list: restrict analysis specific features save_output: set FALSE return plot object without saving files
code: # Define group colors colors <- c("ctrl" = "grey", "sl1" = "#fdcdac", "le1" = "#b3e2cd")  # Basic PCA PCAplot(mmo, color = colors, outdir = '260320_demo/plot/PCA/PCA_basic',         save_output = TRUE)  # PCA with log-normalized data, no sample labels PCAplot(mmo, color = colors, outdir = '260320_demo/plot/PCA/PCA_log',         label = FALSE, normalization = 'Log', save_output = TRUE)  # PCA of flavonoids only PCAplot(mmo, color = colors, outdir = '260320_demo/plot/PCA/PCA_FLV',         label = FALSE, filter_id = TRUE, id_list = FLVs, save_output = TRUE)

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: plot-generating functions eCOMET return list containing plot object, data frames, statistical results. can use create plots aesthetics.
code: # Get PCA plot data pca_res <- PCAplot(mmo, color = colors, outdir = '260320_demo/plot/PCA/PCA_custom',                    save_output = FALSE)  # Access the components # pca_res$plot      — the ggplot object # pca_res$df        — data.frame with PC1, PC2, group, sample columns # pca_res$permanova — PERMANOVA results  # Create a custom plot library(ggplot2) custom_pca <- ggplot(pca_res$df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = group)) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_color_manual(values = colors) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Custom PCA Plot")  ggsave("260320_demo/plot/PCA/PCA_custom.pdf", custom_pca, width = 8, height = 6)

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: PLS-DA (Partial Least Squares Discriminant Analysis) supervised method maximizes separation groups. Unlike PCA, PLS-DA uses group labels dimensionality reduction.
code: PLSDAplot(mmo, color = colors, outdir = '260320_demo/plot/PLSDA/PLSDA_basic.pdf',           save_output = TRUE) PLSDAplot(mmo, color = colors, outdir = '260320_demo/plot/PLSDA/PLSDA_meancentered.pdf',           normalization = 'Meancentered', save_output = TRUE)

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: central goal treatment-based studies find differentially accumulated metabolites (DAMs) — features whose abundance changes significantly treatment control.

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: PairwiseComp() performs pairwise t-test two groups computes: log2 fold change — magnitude change (log2(group2_mean / group1_mean)) adjusted p-value — significance BH correction Convention: first group (group1) reference/control. Positive log2FC means feature higher group2 (treatment).
code: # Compare each treatment to control mmo <- PairwiseComp(mmo, group1 = 'ctrl', group2 = 'sl1') mmo <- PairwiseComp(mmo, group1 = 'ctrl', group2 = 'le1')

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: GetDAMs() extracts DAMs comparisons based fold-change p-value cutoffs. fc_cutoff = 0.5849625 corresponds log2(1.5) — 1.5-fold change pval_cutoff = 0.1 used capture DAMs small demo dataset
code: DAMs <- GetDAMs(mmo, fc_cutoff = 0.5849625, pval_cutoff = 0.1) DAMs_up   <- DAMs$DAMs_up    # Features upregulated in treatment DAMs_down <- DAMs$DAMs_down  # Features downregulated in treatment head(DAMs_up)

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: volcano plot visualizes features fold change (x-axis) significance (y-axis). Features exceeding thresholds highlighted DAMs.
code: VolcanoPlot(mmo, comp = 'ctrl_vs_sl1',             outdir = '260320_demo/plot/Volcano/Volcano_ctrl_vs_sl1.pdf',             save_output = TRUE)  # Remove feature labels by setting topk = 0 VolcanoPlot(mmo, comp = 'ctrl_vs_le1',             outdir = '260320_demo/plot/Volcano/Volcano_ctrl_vs_le1.pdf',             topk = 0, save_output = TRUE)

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: plots visualize overlap DAMs comparisons — e.g., upregulated features shared herbivore treatments?
code: # Define input list for Venn/UpSet VennInput <- list(   sl1.up = DAMs_up$ctrl_vs_sl1.up,   le1.up = DAMs_up$ctrl_vs_le1.up )  # Venn diagram library(ggvenn) venn_plot <- ggvenn(VennInput, stroke_size = 0.5, set_name_size = 4,                     show_percentage = FALSE) +   theme(legend.position = "none") ggsave("260320_demo/plot/Venn/Venn_Upreg.pdf", venn_plot, height = 5, width = 5)  # UpSet plot library(UpSetR) pdf("260320_demo/plot/Upset/Upset_Upreg.pdf", 7, 5) upset(fromList(VennInput), nsets = 10, nintersects = 20,       order.by = 'freq', mainbar.y.label = 'Features in Set',       line.size = 1, point.size = 4, shade.color = 'white',       text.scale = 1, show.numbers = FALSE) dev.off()

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: Heatmaps visualize relative abundance features across samples groups. Features can clustered using hierarchical clustering (default) chemical distances (e.g., DreaMS), following concept Qemistree (Tripathi et al., 2021, Nat Chem Biol). Key options GenerateHeatmapInputs(): Important: using distance, wrap pheatmap() pdf()/dev.(). Instead, use pheatmap(filename = "output.pdf") avoid side-effect plot contamination.

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
text: Note Inf values: Fold-change calculations can produce Inf -Inf feature zero abundance control group (log2(x / 0) = Inf). cap maximum finite fold-change value plotting.
code: library(pheatmap)  # Generate heatmap inputs using fold change and DreaMS chemical distance heatmap_inputs <- GenerateHeatmapInputs(   mmo,   summarize     = 'fold_change',   control_group = 'ctrl',   normalization = 'None',   distance      = 'dreams' )  # Handle Inf/NaN in fold-change matrix (from features absent in control) fc_mat <- as.matrix(heatmap_inputs$FC_matrix) finite_max <- max(abs(fc_mat[is.finite(fc_mat)]), na.rm = TRUE) fc_mat[is.infinite(fc_mat) & fc_mat > 0] <-  finite_max fc_mat[is.infinite(fc_mat) & fc_mat < 0] <- -finite_max fc_mat[is.nan(fc_mat)] <- 0  # Plot heatmap — use filename argument to avoid PDF contamination pheatmap(   mat = fc_mat,   clustering_distance_rows = heatmap_inputs$dist_matrix,   clustering_method = "average",   cellwidth    = 100,   cellheight   = 0.3,   treeheight_row = 100,   fontsize_row = 3,   fontsize_col = 15,   scale        = 'none',   filename     = "260320_demo/plot/Heatmap/Heatmap_FC_dreams.pdf",   width = 10, height = 10 )

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics
code: heatmap_inputs_z <- GenerateHeatmapInputs(   mmo,   summarize     = 'mean',   normalization = 'Z',   distance      = 'dreams' )  # Omit clustering_distance_rows to use default euclidean clustering pheatmap(   mat = heatmap_inputs_z$FC_matrix,   clustering_method = "average",   cellwidth    = 100,   cellheight   = 0.3,   treeheight_row = 100,   fontsize_row = 3,   fontsize_col = 15,   scale        = 'none',   filename     = "260320_demo/plot/Heatmap/Heatmap_Mean_Z_clustering.pdf",   width = 10, height = 10 )

title: Tutorial 1: Treatment-based Metabolomics

what: 3. Compare feature richness across samples and species
title: Tutorial 2: Interspecific Ecometabolomics
text: begin direct question: many metabolite features observed sample species? alpha diversity question summarizing chemical diversity within samples groups rather comparing composition . can use GetAlphaDiversity() calculate several diversity indices data. function based functional Hill framework, means can represent simple richness well measures incorporate feature abundances structural relationships among compounds. three parts GetAlphaDiversity() especially important understand. First, mode argument controls diversity metric calculated. mode = "richness" counts detected features, mode = "unweighted" calculates Hill diversity without structural weighting, mode = "weighted" calculates functional Hill diversity using distance matrix, mode = "faith" calculates Faith-style diversity measure using supplied feature relationship matrix. Second, output argument controls level results summarized. tutorial use sample-level values, group averages, group-cumulative richness, rarefied sample-based summaries. Third, normalization argument tells function abundance table stored mmo object used calculation. simple richness, effectively asking: many distinct features present? setting, analysis ignores structural similarity among compounds focuses whether feature detected sample.

what: A note on controlling feature abundance in GetBetaDiversity()
title: Tutorial 2: Interspecific Ecometabolomics
text: method GetBetaDiversity() treats feature abundance differently, levers available depend method using. Bray-Curtis (method = "bray"): Abundance used directly dissimilarity formula. control much influence high-abundance features normalization argument. normalization = "None" uses raw peak areas, normalization = "Log" compresses dynamic range abundant features less outsized influence, normalization = "PA" converts everything presence/absence abundance ignored entirely. Jaccard (method = "jaccard"): Jaccard presence/absence measure definition. normalization argument ignored — function always uses presence/absence table internally issue warning supply anything else. means Jaccard answers question: two samples detect compounds , regardless much compound present? CSCS (method = "CSCS"): Like Bray-Curtis, CSCS sensitive abundance default control sensitivity normalization. key difference Bray-Curtis CSCS also uses compound distance matrix, structurally related features identical can still contribute similarity. normalization = "None" makes highly abundant features drive CSCS score; normalization = "PA" makes purely structural comparison based chemical neighborhoods represented. Generalized UniFrac (method = "Gen.Uni"): method computes three distance matrices single pass, different abundance-weighting level controlled internal alpha parameter. Rather choosing one upfront, GetBetaDiversity() returns three named list can compare choose appropriate one: d_0: presence/absence — equivalent unweighted UniFrac. Use want know whether chemical branches represented, regardless much compound present. d_0.5: balanced weighting (recommended starting point). Moderates influence highly abundant features without ignoring abundance entirely. d_1: fully abundance-weighted. Dominant features drive distance. Use peak intensity reliable biological signal want abundant compounds influence. present two structure-aware methods :

what: 1. Load data and build the mmo object
title: Tutorial 3: Compound Dendrograms and Molecular Networks
text: Point eCOMET four input files build mmo object one block. Everything downstream reads object. block: mmo$feature_data — feature × sample abundance matrix mmo$metadata — sample group assignments mmo$sirius_annot — CANOPUS class predictions per feature mmo$dreams.dissim — pairwise chemical dissimilarity matrix among features
code: data_dir <- system.file("extdata/tutorials/interspecific", package = "ecomet") stopifnot(nzchar(data_dir))  # fail loudly if package data is missing  demo_feature          <- file.path(data_dir, "Ecomet_Interspecific_Demo_full_feature_table.csv") demo_metadata         <- file.path(data_dir, "Ecomet_Interspecific_Demo_metadata_no_blanks.csv") demo_sirius_formula   <- file.path(data_dir, "canopus_formula_summary.tsv") demo_sirius_structure <- file.path(data_dir, "structure_identifications.tsv") demo_dreams           <- file.path(data_dir, "Ecomet_Interspecific_Demo_dreams_sim_dreams.csv")  # 1a. Feature abundance matrix + metadata mmo <- GetMZmineFeature(   mzmine_dir   = demo_feature,   metadata_dir = demo_metadata,   group_col    = "Species_binomial",   sample_col   = "filename" )  # 1b. SIRIUS/CANOPUS compound class predictions mmo <- AddSiriusAnnot(   mmo,   canopus_structuredir = demo_sirius_structure,   canopus_formuladir   = demo_sirius_formula )  # 1c. DreaMS pairwise chemical distances mmo <- AddChemDist(mmo, dreams_dir = demo_dreams)

text: compound dendrogram groups features pairwise chemical similarity. Features structurally related end nearby branches; features unrelated compound classes end far apart. useful first visualization understanding chemical space covered dataset identifying compound classes well-represented.

what: 3.2 Plot colored by NPC compound class
title: Tutorial 3: Compound Dendrograms and Molecular Networks
text: PlotFeatureDendrogram() takes tree colors tip column SIRIUS annotation table. default column "NPC#pathway", gives broad chemical class label (e.g. Terpenoids, Alkaloids, Shikimates Phenylpropanoids). Features without CANOPUS prediction shown grey. Tip labels hidden default (show_tip_labels = FALSE) feature IDs meaningful glance large tree becomes unreadable . color legend primary way read tree.  want zoom specific class, filter mmo object first rebuild tree: save plot PDF: export tree topology use iTOL FigTree:
code: #need to make this circular   PlotFeatureDendrogram(   tree    = tree_dreams,   mmo     = mmo,   color_by = "NPC#pathway" ) # Terpenoid-only dendrogram terpenoid_ids <- mmo$sirius_annot |>   filter(grepl("Terpenoid", `NPC#pathway`)) |>   pull(id)  tree_terp <- FeatureDendrogram(   mmo,   distance = "dreams",   features = terpenoid_ids )  PlotFeatureDendrogram(   tree     = tree_terp,   mmo      = mmo,   color_by = "NPC#pathway",   main     = "Terpenoid features — DreaMS dendrogram" ) PlotFeatureDendrogram(   tree        = tree_dreams,   mmo         = mmo,   color_by    = "NPC#pathway",   save_output = TRUE,   outprefix   = "output/compound_dendro_dreams" ) tree_dreams <- FeatureDendrogram(   mmo,   distance    = "dreams",   save_newick = TRUE,   outprefix   = "output/compound_dendro_dreams" ) # Writes output/compound_dendro_dreams.nwk

previous_headings: 4. Ion identity networking and the IIN-constrained dendrogram
what: 4.2 Why IIN matters for the dendrogram
title: Tutorial 3: Compound Dendrograms and Molecular Networks
text: plain DreaMS dendrogram section 3, adducts compound appear separate tips. DreaMS similarity computed MS2 spectra adducts compound produce nearly identical fragmentation, usually cluster together anyway — always. Noise, chimeric spectra, missing MS2 can scatter adducts across branches. IIN-constrained dendrogram addresses directly: pairs features within IIN group assigned small fixed distance (within_group_dist = 0.01) clustering. guarantees adducts collapse onto branch, higher-level topology driven genuine chemical differences compounds rather adduct artefacts.

previous_headings: 4. Ion identity networking and the IIN-constrained dendrogram
title: Tutorial 3: Compound Dendrograms and Molecular Networks
text: returned object identical structure plain tree. difference tree_iin$dist_used, within-group pairs set 0.01 hclust ran. tree_iin$tip_map now contains group assignment every feature, PlotFeatureDendrogram() uses draw group highlights.
code: tree_iin <- FeatureDendrogram(   mmo,   distance     = "dreams",   method       = "average",   ion_identity = "ion_identity_network",   iin_col      = "ion_identities:iin_id",  # default — shown here for clarity   within_group_dist = 0.01 ) # Prints: "Ion identity constraint applied: 283 groups, 574 features affected."

Groups span wide gap tree indicate features DreaMS spectra dissimilar despite adduct relationship — worth inspecting individually.

title: Tutorial 3: Compound Dendrograms and Molecular Networks
text: MZmine also assigns feature_group features co-elute show correlated abundance across samples, regardless whether m/z differences match known adducts. broader grouping IIN — dataset 2,283 features fall 740 correlation groups. Correlation groups capture compound across different charge states neutral losses IIN might miss, can also group unrelated features happen co-vary. use : Use ion_identity_network want conservative, chemically validated grouping. confirmed adduct relationships collapsed. Use correlation data lacks IIN annotation want broader grouping includes co-eluting features regardless adduct confirmation. dendrogram iTOL output, IIN generally preferable groups direct chemical interpretation (compound, different ion forms).
code: tree_corr <- FeatureDendrogram(   mmo,   distance     = "dreams",   method       = "average",   ion_identity = "correlation",   corr_col     = "feature_group"  # default — shown here for clarity )  PlotFeatureDendrogram(   tree             = tree_corr,   mmo              = mmo,   color_by         = "NPC#pathway",   highlight_groups = TRUE,   main             = "DreaMS dendrogram — correlation group constraints" )

what: 6. Export a molecular network for Cytoscape
title: Tutorial 3: Compound Dendrograms and Molecular Networks
text: molecular network represents features nodes pairwise chemical similarity edges. Features structurally related connected; isolated features close neighbours. Visualising Cytoscape lets explore chemical space interactively, color nodes compound class abundance, identify clusters related metabolites. ExportCytoscape() generates two files mmo object: _edges.csv — one row per retained edge, source, target, similarity, distance_method columns _nodes.csv — one row per feature, combining abundance statistics available annotations mmo$feature_info mmo$sirius_annot

previous_headings: 6. Export a molecular network for Cytoscape
title: Tutorial 3: Compound Dendrograms and Molecular Networks
text: raw DreaMS distance matrix contains similarity value every feature pair. pairs chemically unrelated appear edges. Two parameters control edges retained: sim_threshold sets minimum similarity floor. pair value excluded regardless many neighbours node . primary lever — raise sparser, higher-confidence network; lower include distant structural neighbours. top_k limits node k similar neighbours threshold filtering. prevents highly-connected hub features overwhelming layout makes network easier read. edge kept falls top-k either endpoint. warning printed retained edge count exceeds 50 000, Cytoscape becomes slow scale.

title: eCOMET-intro
text: mmo object central data structure eCOMET package. created GetMZmineFeature() function contains: feature_data: data frame metabolite features measured values. feature_info: data frame feature definitions include mz rt range grouping information feature grouping applied mzmine. metadata: Sample metadata, including grouping information. sirius_annot: (optional) SIRIUS/CANOPUS class annotations. Added AddSiriusAnnot(). custom_annot: (optional) Custom compound annotations. Added AddCustomAnnot(). cos.dissim: (optional) Cosine dissimilarity matrix. Added AddChemDist(). m2ds.dissim: (optional) MS2DeepScore dissimilarity matrix. Added AddChemDist(). dreams.dissim: (optional) DreaMS dissimilarity matrix. Added AddChemDist(). <name>.dissim: (optional) custom feature dissimilarity matrix. Added AddCustomDist() user-supplied name.

title: eCOMET-intro
text: mendatory input files creating mmo object : MZmine feature table: file generated MZMine (Feature list methods → Export feature list → CSV). See MZMine_documentation Metadata file: CSV file containing sample metadata, following columns. -sample (___.mzML equivalent filename used input MZmine), -mass (sample mass used normalization. just put 1 needed), -least one grouping info column (experimental group sample). Use input ‘group_col’ argument GetMZmineFeature(). Refer Inputs_MZMine details. can create mmo object like : mmo <- GetMZmineFeature( mzmine_dir = “path//mzmine_feature.csv”, metadata_dir = “path//metadata.csv”, group_col = “treatment” ) load demo data downloaded eCOMET installed create mmo object created, can use mmo object normalization, annotation, statistical analysis, visualization throughout package.
code: # Locate tutorial data shipped with the eCOMET package data_dir <- system.file(   "extdata/tutorials/treatment_based",   package = "ecomet" )  stopifnot(nzchar(data_dir))  # fail loudly if package/data not installed  # Define file paths demo_feature <- file.path(data_dir, "feature_table_demo.csv") demo_metadata <- file.path(data_dir, "metadata_demo.csv") demo_sirius_formula <- file.path(data_dir, "canopus_formula_summary.tsv") demo_sirius_structure <- file.path(data_dir, "structure_identifications.tsv") demo_dreams <- file.path(data_dir, "dreams_sim_demo.csv") gls_db <- file.path(data_dir, "custom_DB_glucosinolates.csv")   mmo <- GetMZmineFeature(mzmine_dir=demo_feature, metadata_dir = demo_metadata, group_col = 'group', sample_col = 'sample')

title: eCOMET-intro
text: raw peak area values processed following steps: Replacing missing values (NA) half minimum value feature 1. Normalizing sample mass (provided metadata). Transform data using Log, Mean-centering, Z-score scaling.
code: mmo <- ReplaceZero(mmo, method = 'one') # Replace 0 and NA values by 1 mmo <- MassNormalization(mmo) # Normalize peak area by sample mass in metadata mmo <- MeancenterNormalization(mmo) # Add mean-centered area mmo <- LogNormalization(mmo) # Add log-transformed area mmo <- ZNormalization(mmo) # Add Zscore

what: A Tool for Mass Spectrometry–Based Ecological Metabolomics
title: A Tool for Mass Spectrometry–Based Ecological Metabolomics
text: eCOMET R package designed specifically ecological metabolomics (ecometabolomics), researchers apply untargeted mass spectrometry-based metabolomics characterize chemical phenotypes organisms ecological evolutionary context. Unlike metabolomics biomedical model-organism research, ecometabolomics frequently involve non-model, non-animal systems genomic resources scarce, reference spectral libraries provide limited coverage, metabolic pathway annotations remain incomplete. Never less, goal package help chemical ecologists gain ecological insights metabolomics data despite contraints. two core goals: Standardize ecometabolomics workflows introducing mmo object, unified data container keeps feature abundances, sample metadata, compound annotations, chemical similarity matrices aligned throughout analysis. eCOMET integrates directly outputs leading metabolomics tools — MZmine, SIRIUS/CANOPUS, DreaMS, GNPS — making easier integrate data steams improves reproducibility. Provide educational resources chemical ecologists annotated tutorials covering common study designs, treatment-based experiments multi-species field comparisons. Unlike biomedical metabolomics toolkits, eCOMET built around questions chemical ecologists actually ask: chemical diversity vary across species habitats? compounds differentially accumulated stress? metabolomic composition relate trait performance data?

title: A Tool for Mass Spectrometry–Based Ecological Metabolomics
text: Data ingestion mmo object - Import MZmine feature tables link sample metadata - Add SIRIUS/CANOPUS compound class annotations - Add pairwise chemical similarity DreaMS, MS2DeepScore, cosine similarity - Add custom annotations -house compound databases - Filter mmo object sample, group, feature list — linked tables update together - Match filter associated MGF spectral files keep MS2 data sync Statistics differential analysis - Differential accumulation analysis (DAMs) pairwise group comparisons - Volcano plots, ANOVA post-hoc tests, log2 fold-change summaries - Metabolite set enrichment analysis (MSEA) using CANOPUS class annotations - Chemical class enrichment analysis across multiple ClassyFire NPC levels - Phenotype association screening — correlate individual features continuous ecological variables (height, herbivory, temperature, etc.) Chemical diversity - Alpha diversity: richness, Faith’s PD, Hill numbers (q = 0, 1, 2), functional Hill numbers weighted chemical distance, rarefaction curves - Beta diversity: Bray-Curtis, Jaccard, CSCS (Chemical Structural Compositional Similarity), Generalized UniFrac using spectral similarity distances - Ordination: PCA, PLS-DA, NMDS, PCoA, hierarchical clustering - Specialization index comparing chemical breadth across species treatments Compound networks dendrograms - Build feature dendrograms pairwise chemical distances (DreaMS, MS2DeepScore, cosine, custom) - Incorporate Ion Identity Networking (IIN) feature correlation constraints dendrogram topology - Visualize dendrograms ggtree — circular rectangular layouts, class coloring, fully layerable ggplot2 - Export iTOL interactive annotation publication-quality circular trees - Export node edge tables Cytoscape molecular network visualization Visualization - plot functions return ggplot/ggtree objects — add layers, themes, annotations call - Stacked bar plots NPC compound class composition group - Heatmaps flexible normalization annotation tracks

Contributions welcome — see Contributing page get involved.

text: mmo mmo object dist_matrix square numeric matrix pairwise feature dissimilarities (see Details format requirements). name short string used identify distance downstream functions (e.g. "tanimoto", "npc"). matrix stored mmo$<name>.dissim referenced passing distance = "<name>" GetBetaDiversity(), GetAlphaDiversity(), etc. Must conflict existing names: "dreams", "cosine", "m2ds".

title: Add a custom feature distance matrix to the mmo object — AddCustomDist
text: mmo object new distance matrix stored mmo$<name>.dissim.

title: Add a custom feature distance matrix to the mmo object — AddCustomDist
text: Required matrix format: square numeric matrix equal row column names. Row column names must feature IDs matching id column mmo$feature_data (e.g. "1234.56_2.34" whatever identifier MZmine assigned). Values must dissimilarities range [0, 1], 0 means identical 1 means maximally different. source data similarities (e.g. Tanimoto coefficients, cosine scores), convert first dist_matrix <- 1 - sim_matrix. diagonal 0 (feature identical ). matrix symmetric (dist[,j] == dist[j,]). Non-symmetric matrices accepted may produce unexpected results downstream ordination Hill number calculations. Features present mmo$feature_data retained matrix silently ignored analysis. Features mmo$feature_data absent matrix excluded structure-aware calculations.

title: Add a custom feature distance matrix to the mmo object — AddCustomDist

title: ExportCytoscape — ExportCytoscape
text: Invisibly returns named list: nodes (data.frame), edges (data.frame), nodes_path, edges_path (file paths).

title: ExportCytoscape — ExportCytoscape
text: Edge filtering: Two complementary filters control edges retained. applied together (pair must pass included): sim_threshold – drop edges similarity value. Higher values produce sparser, higher-confidence networks. top_k – node, retain top_k similar neighbours (similarity). NULL keeps edges pass threshold. useful preventing highly-connected hub nodes dominating layout. warning issued retained edge count exceeds 50 000, large networks can slow render Cytoscape. Node table columns: node table always includes id, prevalence (proportion samples detected abundance), one mean_<group> column per group. columns present mmo$feature_info mmo$sirius_annot appended automatically – function assume specific column names. Column names sanitised replace characters cause problems Cytoscape (spaces, #, :). Loading Cytoscape: File -> Import -> Network File -> select _edges.csv. Map source Source Node, target Target Node, similarity Edge Attribute. File -> Import -> Table File -> select _nodes.csv. Map id Key column matching node names. title: GetAlphaDiversity — GetAlphaDiversity
text: Calculate alpha diversity mmo object flexible output modes. Supported diversity modes: 'weighted'   : functional Hill number (GetFunctionalHillNumber) 'unweighted' : Hill numbers abundances (GetHillNumbers) 'faith'      : Faith's phylogenetic diversity (GetFaithPD) 'richness'   : simple feature richness (GetRichness)

title: GetAlphaDiversity — GetAlphaDiversity
code: GetAlphaDiversity(   mmo,   q = 1,   normalization = "None",   mode = "richness",   distance = "dreams",   threshold = 0,   filter_id = FALSE,   id_list = NULL,   filter_group = FALSE,   group_list = NULL,   output = c("sample_level", "group_average", "group_cumulative", "rarefied_sample"),   group_col = "group",   sample_col = "sample",   pool_method = c("sum", "mean"),   n_perm = 200,   ci = 0.95,   seed = NULL )

title: GetAlphaDiversity — GetAlphaDiversity
text: mmo mmo object containing feature data metadata q Hill number order controlling abundance sensitivity (default: 1). applies mode = "weighted" mode = "unweighted"; ignored "richness" "faith". q = 0 – richness: detected features count equally regardless abundance. q = 1 – Shannon-type: features weighted proportionally relative abundance. q = 2 – Simpson-type: dominant (high-abundance) features weighted strongly. normalization Abundance table use. Options: 'None', 'Log', 'Meancentered', 'Z', 'PA' (default: 'None'). Using 'PA' forces presence/absence regardless mode q, effectively making every detected feature equally abundant Hill calculation. mode diversity metric calculate. One 'weighted', 'unweighted', 'faith', 'richness' (default: 'richness'). Use q control abundance sensitivity 'weighted' 'unweighted'. distance Feature dissimilarity metric: 'dreams', 'm2ds', 'cosine' (default: 'dreams'). Required mode = "weighted" mode = "faith"; ignored otherwise. threshold Numeric threshold used define metabolite presence (default: 0) filter_id boolean indicating whether filter feature data specific list (default: FALSE) id_list list feature names filter feature data , filter_id TRUE (default: NULL) filter_group boolean indicating whether filter feature data specific group list (default: FALSE) group_list list groups filter feature data , filter_group TRUE (default: NULL) output Output mode: 'sample_level', 'group_average', 'group_cumulative', 'rarefied_sample' group_col Column mmo$metadata defines groups (default: 'group') sample_col Column mmo$metadata defines sample IDs (default: 'sample') pool_method pool abundances combining samples: 'sum' 'mean' (default: 'sum') n_perm Integer; maximum number permutations per rarefaction level (default: 200) ci Numeric; confidence level (default: 0.95) seed Optional integer seed reproducibility (default: NULL)

title: GetAlphaDiversity — GetAlphaDiversity
text: output != 'rarefied_sample': data.frame. output = 'rarefied_sample': list : summary: group-level rarefaction summary (mean, lwr, upr, n_perm_eff) raw: permutation-level values group n_samples

title: GetAlphaDiversity — GetAlphaDiversity
text: Output modes control samples handled: 'sample_level'     : alpha per sample -> returns sample, group, value 'group_average'    : mean alpha per group (summarize sample_level) -> group, mean, sd, se, n, lwr, upr 'group_cumulative' : pooled gamma per group (pool samples within group) -> group, value 'rarefied_sample'  : sample-based rarefaction within group (subsample N samples, pool, compute) -> group, n_samples, mean, lwr, upr, n_perm NOTE: outputs 3 4, pooling performed summing feature intensities across samples.

title: GetBetaDiversity — GetBetaDiversity
text: Calculate beta diversity (sample--sample dissimilarity) mmo object. Four methods supported, differing handle feature abundance structural relationships among compounds:

title: GetBetaDiversity — GetBetaDiversity
code: GetBetaDiversity(   mmo,   method = "Gen.Uni",   normalization = "None",   distance = NULL,   filter_id = FALSE,   id_list = NULL,   filter_group = FALSE,   group_list = NULL,   scale_dissim = TRUE )

title: GetBetaDiversity — GetBetaDiversity
text: mmo mmo object containing feature data metadata method Beta diversity method: 'Gen.Uni', 'bray', 'jaccard', 'CSCS' (default: 'Gen.Uni') normalization Abundance table use. Options: 'None', 'Log', 'Meancentered', 'Z', 'PA' (default: 'None'). Ignored 'jaccard', always uses PA. 'bray' 'CSCS', primary lever controlling abundance sensitivity. distance Feature dissimilarity metric: 'dreams', 'm2ds', 'cosine'. Required 'Gen.Uni' 'CSCS'; ignored 'bray' 'jaccard'. filter_id boolean indicating whether filter feature data specific list (default: FALSE) id_list list feature names filter feature data , filter_id TRUE (default: NULL) filter_group boolean indicating whether filter feature data specific group list (default: FALSE) group_list list groups filter feature data , filter_group TRUE (default: NULL) scale_dissim Boolean; whether scale feature distance matrix 0,1 (default: TRUE)

title: GetBetaDiversity — GetBetaDiversity
text: 'bray', 'jaccard', 'CSCS': symmetric sample--sample distance matrix (matrix). 'Gen.Uni': named list three distance matrices named d_0 (presence/absence), d_0.5 (balanced), d_1 (fully abundance-weighted).

title: GetBetaDiversity — GetBetaDiversity
text: 'bray'    : Bray-Curtis dissimilarity. Feature-overlap ; compound distance matrix required. Abundance sensitivity controlled via normalization: use 'None' raw abundances, 'Log' compress dynamic range, 'PA' reduce presence/absence. 'jaccard' : Jaccard dissimilarity. Presence/absence definition. normalization argument ignored; PA data always used internally. warning issued non-PA normalization supplied. 'CSCS'    : Chemical Structural Compositional Similarity. Incorporates pairwise compound distances samples sharing structurally related (identical) features can still considered similar. Requires distance. Abundance sensitivity controlled via normalization (options bray). 'Gen.Uni' : Generalized UniFrac. Partitions beta diversity across branches compound dendrogram. Requires distance. Returns named list three distance matrices, one per abundance-weighting level (d_0, d_0.5, d_1). Choose one appropriate analysis (see Details). Choosing abundance-weighting level Gen.Uni: d_0   – presence/absence ; equivalent unweighted UniFrac. Use detection (intensity) signal interest. d_0.5 – balanced weighting. Moderates influence highly abundant features without ignoring abundance. d_1   – fully abundance-weighted. Dominant features drive distance. Use peak intensity reliable biological signal. Access specific matrix e.g. result[["d_0.5"]].

title: GetBetaDiversity — GetBetaDiversity

text: tree Output list FeatureDendrogram(). mmo mmo object. Used mmo$sirius_annot color_by NULL. color_by Column name mmo$sirius_annot use tip point colors (default: "NPC#pathway"). Set NULL skip tip coloring. layout Tree layout passed ggtree::ggtree(). One "circular" (default), "rectangular", "fan", "slanted", "radial", layout supported ggtree. color_branches Logical; whether color tree branches annotation color_by, addition tip points (default: FALSE). Branch color determined majority-vote tip annotations descending node, mixed-class clades colored whichever class abundant. Unannotated mixed branches fall back na_color. highlight_groups Logical; whether highlight ion identity / correlation groups using ggtree::geom_hilight() (default: TRUE). effect tree$tip_map NULL. palette Qualitative palette name colorspace::qualitative_hcl (default: "Dark 3"). na_color Color tips/branches annotation (default: "grey70"). tip_size Point size tip points (default: 1). branch_size Line width tree branches (default: 0.5). Increase small trees; decrease large ones. show_tip_labels Logical; draw feature ID text tips (default: FALSE). useful small trees. open_angle layout = "fan", opening angle degrees (default: 10).

title: PlotFeatureDendrogram — PlotFeatureDendrogram
text: ggtree/ggplot object. Add layers +.

title: PlotFeatureDendrogram — PlotFeatureDendrogram
text: Design philosophy: function returns ggtree object can keep layering modifications call: Tip point coloring (color_by): metabolomics trees hundreds thousands tips, text labels rarely readable. Instead, tiny colored points drawn tip using ggtree::geom_tippoint() indicate compound class. Use show_tip_labels = TRUE additionally draw feature ID text (recommended large trees). IIN / correlation group highlighting (highlight_groups): tree$tip_map present, ion identity / correlation group highlighted using ggtree::geom_hilight(), shades MRCA clade members group.
code: p <- PlotFeatureDendrogram(tree, mmo)   p + ggtree::geom_tiplab(size = 1.5) +       ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: PlotFeatureDendrogram — PlotFeatureDendrogram
code: if (FALSE) { tree <- FeatureDendrogram(mmo, distance = "dreams")  # Basic circular tree coloured by NPC pathway (tip points only) PlotFeatureDendrogram(tree, mmo)  # Color branches too, thicker lines for a small tree PlotFeatureDendrogram(tree, mmo, color_branches = TRUE, branch_size = 1.2)  # Rectangular layout, no coloring PlotFeatureDendrogram(tree, mmo, layout = "rectangular", color_by = NULL)  # Returned object is a ggplot -- keep layering p <- PlotFeatureDendrogram(tree, mmo) p + ggtree::geom_tiplab(size = 1, align = TRUE) }

text: Applies minimum-probability cutoff selected CANOPUS (NPClassifier / ClassyFire) annotation levels inside ecomet mmo object. function reads chosen annotation table mmo[[input]] (default "sirius_annot"), flags annotations threshold setting NA, stores result new element mmo named "sirius_annot_filtered_<suffix>".

title: Filter CANOPUS / SIRIUS annotations in an ecomet mmo object by probability threshold — filter_canopus_annotations
text: mmo ecomet mmo object containing mmo[[input]] (data.frame). input Character. Name element mmo filter. Defaults "sirius_annot". pathway_level Character vector one annotation header(s) filter. Valid options include: "" "All_NPC" "All_ClassyFire" "NPC#pathway" "NPC#superclass" "NPC#class" "ClassyFire#superclass" "ClassyFire#class" "ClassyFire#subclass" "ClassyFire#level 5" "ClassyFire#specific class" Special values: "" expands NPC + ClassyFire levels listed . "All_NPC" expands NPC levels . "All_ClassyFire" expands ClassyFire levels . threshold Decimal 0 1. Annotations probability < threshold flagged NA. na_prob Logical. TRUE (default), also set corresponding probability value NA. suffix Optional character string appended created element name: "sirius_annot_filtered_<suffix>". NULL (default), suffix auto-generated. overwrite Logical. FALSE (default) target element already exists mmo, function errors avoid accidental overwrites. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints concise summary including counts non-missing annotations filtering.

title: Filter CANOPUS / SIRIUS annotations in an ecomet mmo object by probability threshold — filter_canopus_annotations
text: updated mmo object, new element mmo[[paste0("sirius_annot_filtered_", suffix)]].

title: Filter an mmo object by samples, groups, and/or features — filter_mmo
text: mmo list-like mmo object returned GetMZmineFeature(). sample_list Optional character vector sample IDs (matching sample_col mmo$metadata) retain. group_list Optional character vector group labels (matching group_col mmo$metadata) retain. Mutually exclusive sample_list. id_list Optional character vector feature IDs retain. NULL, features determined feature_data optionally filtered drop_empty_feat. sample_col Column name mmo$metadata containing sample IDs. Passed filter_mgf_to_mmo(). NULL (default), output "<input>_filtered.mgf".
